deployment_question: How does the model generalize to genomic regions or chromosomes absent from training?
definition: Hold out all examples from one chromosome or genomic region while training on the remaining chromosomes or regions.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: sets same-chromosome overlap to zero and reduces locus-specific leakage

implementation_recipe: Assign complete chromosomes or large genomic regions to test folds.
Report whether homologous/repetitive sequences remain across chromosomes.
quality_gates: Prevent locus-specific average leakage across cell types or assays.
Report genomic distance and sequence similarity to training examples.
Check label balance across held-out chromosomes.
failure_modes: Chromosome identity is coarse and can confound gene density or assay coverage.
Repeats or paralogous sequence motifs can leak across chromosomes.
A held-out chromosome can have too few positives.

citations.title: LOCO-EPI: Leave-one-chromosome-out as a benchmarking paradigm
citations.evidence: Introduces LOCO evaluation for enhancer-promoter interaction prediction and reports standard methods overestimate performance.

citations.title: A pitfall for machine learning methods aiming to predict across cell types
citations.evidence: Shows models can memorize locus-specific averages, motivating genomic-region holdouts.
